description: Use early in ideation or when stuck generating ideas.

Orchestrate multi-agent discussions when you need multiple perspectives or want agents to collaborate. Can use shared BMAD planning capability rounds: capability:zoom-out, capability:tdd, capability:ubiquitous-language, and capability:grill-me.

Run a Capability Improvement Toolkit planning prompt for likely architecture boundary drift. Use when code or plans may violate intended architecture boundaries and need drift findings, evidence, impact, and fix path before implementation.
Run a Capability Improvement Toolkit planning prompt to decide use, skip, or enhance for existing BMAD skills, repo scripts, MCPs, browser affordances, and automation before new work.
Run the highest-leverage-official-mcp-addition prompt for adding only official MCPs that beat existing scripts, docs, skills, or manual evidence.

Plan and route safe best-practice refactors for capability/plugin code using Forge artifacts and local evidence refs. Prompt-only v1; no live tools, file edits, commits, deploys, _bmad/custom writes, Workspace authority, or verifier authority.

Plan language-agnostic code optimization refactors using local evidence refs, measurement before change, public behavior preservation checks, and smallest safe optimization phases. Prompt-only; no live profiling, file edits, commands, commits, deploys, or runtime authority.

Generate draft BMAD capability-pack artifacts from local evidenceRefs, Capability Request JSON, and optional advisory evidence sources. Use when a tool capability needs reviewable pack artifacts without live tools, Workspace command changes, verifier authority, or _bmad/custom authority.

Run generic local Codex/BMAD loop contract for one-shot or recurring WorkflowBundle goals with explicit goal input, capability:zoom-out, capability:tdd, capability:ubiquitous-language, capability:grill-me, branch safety, quality gates, install/refresh evidence, and checkpointed continuation; does not run Implementation Readiness by default.

Run BMAD repository improvement as thin predefined bmad-loop instance, not a separate engine. Requires explicit operator goal, goal_ref, or scope; inherits unresolved LoopRunConfig fields from bmad-loop; consumes capability:zoom-out, capability:tdd, capability:ubiquitous-language, and capability:grill-me; preserves SI-AUTO aliases.

Orient users and recommend next BMAD steps by routing by phase/intent/catalog, including shared BMAD planning capabilities when intent fits: capability:zoom-out, capability:tdd, capability:ubiquitous-language, and capability:grill-me.

Operate BMAD Workspace Sessions from Codex for session setup, packets, evidence, result/review/closeout ledger, Review Manifest, archives, archive diff, grant-governed base improvement, and shared planning setup refs for capability:zoom-out, capability:tdd, capability:ubiquitous-language, and capability:grill-me; no runtime authority, readiness gate, or override authority.
Use when LLM needs to understand available docs without loading everything.
Use when doc becomes too large (>500 lines) to manage effectively.
Use after drafting to polish written content.
output-location: three-column markdown table with suggested fixes

description: Use when doc produced from multiple subprocesses or needs structural improvement.
Use for quality assurance or before finalizing deliverables. Code Review in other modules runs this automatically, but also useful for document reviews.
Use alongside adversarial review for orthogonal coverage — method-driven not attitude-driven.
Use when you need token-efficient distillates that preserve all information for downstream LLM consumption.
required: adjacent to source document or specified output_path

description: Use when you want to author sparse [workflow] or [agent] overrides for exposed fields such as facts, hooks, templates, and defaults. It writes _bmad/custom/ overrides and verifies the merge; never grants verifier authority, grants, runtime bypass, or safety bypass.
